chief_complaint: A 37-year-old female presented with right upper abdominal pain.
history: The patient had a background of undiagnosed genetic hypertriglyceridaemia. She developed hypertriglyceridaemia pancreatitis, which progressed to atypical haemolytic uraemic syndrome (aHUS) and secondary haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (sHLH).
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination: not provided.
labs: Elevated triglycerides and lipase levels. Rhabdomyolysis, haemolysis, progressive renal failure, thrombocytopenia, and unremitting fevers.
correct_diagnosis: Hypertriglyceridaemic pancreatitis associated with atypical haemolytic uraemic syndrome and secondary haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis
differentials: Hypertriglyceridaemic pancreatitis associated with atypical haemolytic uraemic syndrome and secondary haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis
correct_ranking: Hypertriglyceridaemic pancreatitis associated with atypical haemolytic uraemic syndrome and secondary haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis
